diagrams.title: DP Problem Identification Flowchart
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing how to identify if a problem can be solved using Dynamic Programming
elements: Step 3: If both YES → Use DP
Step 4: If only optimal substructure → Consider Greedy
Step 5: If independent subproblems → Use Divide and Conquer
diagrams.purpose: Helps identify when to apply Dynamic Programming to a problem

diagrams.title: Memoization vs Tabulation Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of top-down and bottom-up DP approaches with execution flow
elements: Top-Down (Memoization): Problem → Check Cache → Compute if needed → Store → Return
Bottom-Up (Tabulation): Base Cases → Build Table Iteratively → Use Table for Final Answer
Arrows showing recursive vs iterative nature
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the two main approaches to implement Dynamic Programming

diagrams.title: 0/1 Knapsack DP Table
diagrams.description: 2D table showing how values are filled for knapsack problem with 4 items and capacity 5
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how the knapsack DP table is constructed and interpreted

diagrams.title: LCS Backtracking Path
diagrams.description: LCS table for strings showing the backtracking path to find the actual subsequence
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how to trace back to find the actual Longest Common Subsequence
